questions.prompt: What does 'register' refer to in linguistics?
options: The volume at which someone speaks
The variety of language used in a particular social situation
The grammatical tense used in a sentence
The regional accent of a speaker
questions.explanation: Register refers to the variety of language appropriate to a particular social situation — ranging from colloquial to highly formal.

questions.prompt: Which connector signals addition or reinforcement?
questions.explanation: 'Asimismo' means 'likewise / also / furthermore' and signals addition. 'No obstante' signals contrast, 'por ende' signals consequence, and 'dado que' signals cause.

questions.prompt: 'Aunque salga temprano, llegaré tarde.' This sentence means:
options: Even though I leave early, I will arrive late. (known fact)
Even if I leave early, I will arrive late. (hypothetical)
Since I leave early, I will arrive late.
Unless I leave early, I will arrive late.
questions.explanation: 'Aunque + subjunctive' (salga) expresses a hypothetical concession — 'even if I leave early' (it hasn't happened yet, or it's uncertain). The subjunctive marks it as hypothetical.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of hedging language?
options: Esta es claramente la única solución.
En cierta medida, todos somos responsables.
El problema es grave y debe resolverse ya.
questions.explanation: 'En cierta medida' (to a certain extent) is a hedging expression that softens a claim, acknowledging it is not absolute.

questions.prompt: When is the true passive ('fue firmado por') preferred over impersonal 'se'?
options: When the agent (doer) is important or must be named
questions.explanation: The true passive is preferred when naming the agent matters. 'Se firmó el acuerdo' omits the agent; 'El acuerdo fue firmado por el presidente' names who did it.

questions.prompt: What grammatical mood always follows 'de ahí que'?
questions.explanation: 'De ahí que' always triggers the subjunctive: 'De ahí que sea necesario actuar.'

questions.prompt: Which technique creates emphasis by delaying the main point to the end of the sentence?
questions.explanation: A periodic sentence builds tension by presenting background information first and delivering the main clause at the end — a technique that creates rhetorical impact.

questions.prompt: Which newspaper is mentioned in the lesson as a standard for neutral journalistic Mexican Spanish?
questions.explanation: The lesson mentions 'Reforma' and 'El Universal' as newspapers that set the standard for neutral journalistic prose in Mexico.
